skill.name: blind-review-sanitizer
skill.description: Structured manuscript anonymization for double-blind peer review. Removes author names, affiliations, contact details, acknowledgments, and self-citations from .docx, .md, and .txt files while documenting assumptions and remaining risks.
skill.summary: Remove author-identifying content from academic manuscripts for double-blind peer review submission.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 61 static analyzer findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections matched on filename substrings (e.g., 'MD' in SKILL.md) - no cryptographic operations exist in any file. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections are markdown code block formatting in documentation, not executable code. The 'path traversal' finding is a security checklist item prohibiting path traversal. The 'system reconnaissance' findings matched on common words like 'scope' and 'system' in audit report text. The 'unicode escape sequence' finding is a legitimate CJK character range in regex. The only actual behaviors are: running a local Python script to process manuscript files (declared purpose), reading input files and writing anonymized output (declared purpose), and a single source attribution URL in SKILL.md (no network calls in code). No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, obfuscation, or malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Local filesystem read and write operations
low_findings.description: The Python script reads manuscript input files and writes anonymized output files to the local filesystem. This is the declared purpose of the skill and uses user-specified paths only. No path traversal or unauthorized access patterns exist.

low_findings.title: Documentation shell command examples flagged as execution risks
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md and references/audit-reference.md files contain shell command examples in markdown code blocks (e.g., 'python scripts/main.py --help'). These are documentation showing intended usage, not embedded execution. The Python script itself contains no subprocess, os.system, or shell execution calls.

low_findings.title: Source attribution URL incorrectly flagged as network risk
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 7 contains a GitHub URL linking to the source repository. This is a static attribution link, not a runtime network call. The Python code makes no network requests and imports no networking libraries.

content.user_title: Anonymize Manuscripts for Double-Blind Peer Review
content.value_statement: Academic manuscripts often contain author-identifying information that must be removed before double-blind peer review. This skill provides structured anonymization by removing author names, affiliations, contact details, acknowledgments, and self-citations while documenting assumptions and remaining risks.
actual_capabilities: Remove author names and affiliations from .docx, .md, and .txt manuscripts
Detect and replace self-citations with neutral placeholders such as [PREVIOUS WORK]
Remove or preserve acknowledgment sections based on user preference
Generate a summary of all identifying items removed during anonymization
Provide a manual review checklist for unsupported file formats like PDF
Document assumptions and remaining deanonymization risks in the output
limitations: Cannot process PDF files or scanned documents with automated anonymization
File metadata and embedded images require separate manual review
Requires Python 3.10 or higher and python-docx library for .docx file processing
Cannot guarantee complete anonymity without final human review of the output

use_cases.title: Preparing Conference Submissions
use_cases.description: Anonymize a manuscript before submitting to a conference that requires double-blind review. The skill removes all author-identifying content and provides a summary of changes made.

use_cases.title: Reviewing Self-Citation Patterns
use_cases.description: Identify and flag self-citations in a draft paper using the highlight mode. Review each flagged citation manually before deciding whether to remove or keep it.

use_cases.title: Journal Manuscript Preparation
use_cases.description: Remove all author-identifying content from a revised manuscript before resubmission to a journal with double-blind requirements. Produces a sanitized file with documented assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Manuscript Anonymization
prompt_templates.prompt: Anonymize my manuscript at manuscript.txt for double-blind review. Remove all author names, affiliations, and contact information.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user with a simple plain text manuscript and no special options

prompt_templates.title: Selective Anonymization with Known Authors
prompt_templates.prompt: Process my manuscript at paper.md using the author list 'Jane Smith, John Doe' for stronger detection. Keep the acknowledgments section but remove all self-citations.
prompt_templates.scenario: User who knows the full author list and wants to preserve acknowledgments

prompt_templates.title: DOCX Processing with Self-Citation Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Anonymize the file at manuscript.docx. Use --highlight-self-cites to flag self-citations instead of removing them. I want to review each one manually before making changes.
prompt_templates.scenario: User who wants to audit self-citations before any automated removal

prompt_templates.title: Unsupported Format with Manual Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: My manuscript is a PDF at thesis.pdf that I cannot convert. Provide a manual anonymization checklist with all known risk areas for double-blind review preparation.
prompt_templates.scenario: User with an unsupported file format needing structured manual guidance

output_examples.input: Process paper.docx with authors 'John Smith, Jane Doe'
output_examples.output: Output saved to paper-blinded.docx. 12 items processed. Summary: 2 author names replaced, 3 institutions anonymized, 2 emails and 1 phone number removed, acknowledgment section removed. Remaining risks: check figure captions, embedded URLs, and file metadata manually before submission.

output_examples.input: Anonymize manuscript.txt and keep the acknowledgments section
output_examples.output: Output saved to manuscript-blinded.txt. 8 items processed. Summary: author names and institutions replaced with placeholders, contact information removed. Acknowledgments preserved as requested. Manual review still needed for grant numbers and supplementary references.

output_examples.input: My manuscript is a scanned PDF, cannot convert to text
output_examples.output: Automated processing is not available for scanned PDFs. Manual anonymization checklist: (1) Check title page for author names and affiliations, (2) Review page headers and footers for identifying marks, (3) Examine the acknowledgments section, (4) Check reference list for self-citations, (5) Review figure captions and table labels, (6) Inspect file metadata and properties.

best_practices: Provide the full author name list for more accurate detection and replacement of identifying content
Review the anonymized output manually before submission even after automated processing completes
Use the highlight-self-cites flag first to audit self-citations before deciding which ones to remove
anti_patterns: Submitting the anonymized output without performing a manual review of the results
Attempting to process PDF files directly without converting to a supported format first
Assuming the skill handles file metadata and embedded image cleanup in addition to text anonymization

faq.question: What file formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports .docx, .md, and .txt files for automated anonymization. PDF files and scanned documents are not supported for automated processing but a manual checklist can be provided.

faq.question: Do I need to provide author names?
faq.answer: Author names are optional but strongly recommended. Providing them enables the script to detect and replace full names and surnames throughout the manuscript for more thorough anonymization.

faq.question: Can I preserve the acknowledgments section?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --keep-acknowledgments flag to preserve the acknowledgments section while still anonymizing author names and other identifying content in the rest of the manuscript.

faq.question: Is the anonymization process reversible?
faq.answer: No. The skill replaces identifying content with placeholders like [AUTHOR NAME] and [INSTITUTION]. Always keep a copy of your original file before running anonymization.

faq.question: Does the skill remove metadata from files?
faq.answer: No. File metadata, embedded image data, and document properties require separate manual review. The skill only processes visible text content within the manuscript.

faq.question: What Python version and dependencies are required?
faq.answer: Python 3.10 or higher is required. For .docx file processing, the python-docx library must be installed. Plain text and markdown files work with only the Python standard library.
